exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: Experience is what it is like to be there. It has nothing to do with memory. It has nothing to do with the future — not how you could articulate it to your friends.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: In Urdu there's a word for it. The substance of your body, if you imagine it like a sponge — when something is deep in there, it has infused itself into your body.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: The magic happened before you ever started talking to Claude. You saw a problem and thought — it's solvable. That's what possibility is. The how is incidental.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: The money I make has nothing to do with my value, and everything to do with my negotiation.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: Negotiation is all about what the other party thinks your plan B is.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: You don't decenter structures of power by pushing off, or by attaching yourself to them. You decenter them by building other centers of gravity.

exchange.speaker: K
exchange.text: My sense of these things anchors to whatever makes me feel better.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: Don't anchor on a number. Think about a process.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: It's very easy, as people get older, to become resigned. That's the thing I like about you — you never are. You'll figure out a way, and you won't let the world make you bitter.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: Optimization is one-dimensional. This is about living a fuller life. A more expansive one.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: I'm so glad I feel equally violent about my own work as I do about other people's.

exchange.speaker: K
exchange.text: Do you think spaces can be made sacred?

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: I don't feel reverence. I feel wonder, and I feel curiosity.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: Problems of courage are not problems of reasons to leap. They are problems of not leaping.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: Storytelling compresses a multifaceted gem onto a 2D plane. Here's one projection, here's another. But what if you didn't want to compress it?

exchange.speaker: K
exchange.text: You're looking for the one story that unifies them. I think it's time for multiple stories.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: Us working together is a ceiling condition, not a floor condition. I wouldn't take on something where the floor is collapsible — but I'd like us to reach the ceiling.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: Normally, when I would burn out in nine months — with you, I can do two years.

theme: On what we give each other

exchange.speaker: K
exchange.text: It's fair to assume people know how to do work, but not how to work. A good model when you don't have much expertise: build a checklist, until you're at a point you don't need the checklist.

exchange.speaker: K
exchange.text: I'm excited. I'm happy with our lives.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: You can feel anticipatory anxiety too. I can be excited for both of us.

exchange.speaker: K
exchange.text: Is it because it's a breadcrumb?

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: It's the mark you've left on the world. There's something fun about it, in a tiny, playful way.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: All you're trying to do is a V1 of an event, in a format you'd love. It doesn't have to be the best thing people have ever experienced.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: I've always just used this thing you can do as an instrument. My muscle for selling the intangible is weak — but only one of us needs to know how to do that.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: You show up with your availability. But I don't think your availability is the cheapest thing you have. I think it's the most expensive.

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: Expense is not painfulness.

exchange.speaker: K
exchange.text: Then what is expense?

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: If you're spending willpower, or cognitive, or creativity resources on anything — it's expensive. That's why you don't let yourself feel tired after your family. Of course you love them, so how dare it be expensive? But it's also exhausting.

exchange.speaker: K
exchange.text: What stops us from doing that level of quality on our own projects?

exchange.speaker: D
exchange.text: We don't hold the bar for ourselves enough. Individually we're excellent — together, destructive interference. We've got one brain cell working on it.
